2026-05-04T14:38:08.0909847Z       ### 4. Add filtering to MetricsEngine methods
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0910057Z       
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0910331Z       Add `filter: FileFilterSpec = FileFilterSpec()` parameter to these methods in MetricsEngine:
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0910806Z       - `fanInRanking(limit: Int, filter: FileFilterSpec = FileFilterSpec())` - filter on `targetPath`
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0911542Z       - `fanOutRanking(limit: Int, filter: FileFilterSpec = FileFilterSpec())` - filter on `sourcePath`
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0912216Z       - `lowUsageSymbols(maxOccurrences: Int = 2, limit: Int = 50, filter: FileFilterSpec = FileFilterSpec())` - filter on `targetPath`
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0912750Z       - `deadCodeCandidates(filter: FileFilterSpec = FileFilterSpec())` - filter on `path`
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0913254Z       - `changeImpactRadius(fqName: String, depth: Int, filter: FileFilterSpec = FileFilterSpec())` - filter on `sourcePath`
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0913642Z       
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0913782Z       For fanIn:
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0913932Z       ```kotlin
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0914216Z       fun fanInRanking(limit: Int, filter: FileFilterSpec = FileFilterSpec()): List<FanInMetric> {
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0914671Z           // ... existing query ...
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0914985Z           return readMetricRows(...).filterByPath(filter) { it.targetPath }
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0915270Z       ```
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0915424Z       
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0916643Z       Increase the SQL LIMIT to handle post-filter: when a filter is active, fetch `limit * 5` from SQL then post-filter and take(limit). When no filter, use `limit` directly. Actually simpler: always apply filter after, but keep LIMIT in SQL as-is and just add `.filterByPath(filter)` after the query. The limit acts as an upper bound on pre-filter rows so may not return `limit` filtered results, but that's acceptable for now. The `limit` parameter is a hint for how many to return.
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0918378Z       
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0919177Z       Actually for fan-in and fan-out, the query already has `LIMIT ?`. When filtering is active, we should fetch more rows then post-filter. A simple approach: if filter is non-empty, use `limit * FILTER_FETCH_MULTIPLIER` (say 10) as the SQL limit, then post-filter and take `limit`. If filter is empty, use `limit` as SQL limit.
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0920013Z       
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0920617Z       For simplicity, add a `private fun MetricsEngine.effectiveSqlLimit(requestedLimit: Int, filter: FileFilterSpec): Int = if (filter.isEmpty) requestedLimit else requestedLimit * 10` helper, cap at max reasonable value.
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0921238Z       
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0922032Z       Actually looking at the existing code more carefully, the SQL LIMIT is bound via `bind = { setInt(1, limit) }`. We need to change the bind when filtering. Let me make it simpler - just always apply post-filter after, use the requestedLimit for SQL:
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0922960Z       - When `filter.isEmpty` → unchanged behavior
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0923407Z       - When filter is set → SQL still runs with `limit`, then filter is applied (may return fewer than limit)
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0923760Z       
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0924038Z       This is the simplest correct approach. Add a note in docs that filtering is post-SQL.
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0924350Z       
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0924698Z       Add tests in `index-store/src/test/kotlin/io/github/amichne/kast/indexstore/MetricsEngineTest.kt` for the filtering:

2026-05-04T14:38:08.0971674Z     **New `FileFilterSpec.kt`**: `fileGlob: String?` + `folderPrefix: String?`, lazy `PathMatcher` compilation, `matches(path)` method, `isEmpty` guard. Companion `filterByPath<T>` extension short-circuits when empty.
2026-05-04T14:38:08.0972371Z

2026-05-04T14:38:52.4975646Z     import java.nio.file.FileSystems
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4975941Z     
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4976138Z     /**
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4976734Z      * Generic file-path filter for metrics queries.
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4977081Z      *
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4977442Z      * [fileGlob] is a glob pattern applied to the full file path (e.g. `**Test.kt`).
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4978066Z      * [folderPrefix] is a path prefix filter - only files whose path starts with this
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4978590Z      * prefix are included (e.g. `/src/main/kotlin`).
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4978929Z      *
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4979264Z      * Both filters are combined with AND semantics when both are non-null.
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4979679Z      */
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4979906Z     data class FileFilterSpec(
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4980324Z         val fileGlob: String? = null,
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4980542Z         val folderPrefix: String? = null,
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4980828Z     ) {
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4981058Z         private val globMatcher by lazy {
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4981485Z             fileGlob?.let {
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4981928Z                 val pattern = if (it.startsWith("glob:") || it.startsWith("regex:")) it else "glob:$it"
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4982492Z                 FileSystems.getDefault().getPathMatcher(pattern)
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4982863Z             }
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4983078Z         }
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4983280Z     
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4983591Z         /** Returns true if [path] passes all configured filters. */
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4984015Z         fun matches(path: String?): Boolean {
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4984357Z             if (path == null) return false
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4984688Z             if (folderPrefix != null) {
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4985206Z                 val normalized = if (folderPrefix.endsWith("/")) folderPrefix else "$folderPrefix/"
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4985867Z                 if (!path.startsWith(normalized) && path != folderPrefix) return false
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4986300Z             }
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4986538Z             if (globMatcher != null) {
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4986906Z                 val filePath = java.nio.file.Path.of(path)
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4987342Z                 if (!globMatcher!!.matches(filePath)) return false
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4987701Z             }
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4987921Z             return true
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4988171Z         }
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4988378Z     
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4988712Z         val isEmpty: Boolean get() = fileGlob == null && folderPrefix == null
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4989134Z     }
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4989333Z     
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4989790Z     /** Filters a list retaining only items where [pathExtractor] returns a path that passes [filter]. */
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4990576Z     fun <T> List<T>.filterByPath(filter: FileFilterSpec, pathExtractor: (T) -> String?): List<T> =
2026-05-04T14:38:52.4991428Z         if (filter.isEmpty) this else filter { item -> filter.matches(pathExtractor(item)) }

2026-05-04T14:40:08.7428920Z     - In index-store/src/main/kotlin/io/github/amichne/kast/indexstore/FileFilterSpec.kt, lines 29-30: The folder prefix matching logic has a flaw. When `folderPrefix` is `/src/main` and `path` is `/src/main`, the code checks `path.startsWith("/src/main/")` (false) and `path == "/src/main"` (true), so it passes. However, when `path` is `/src/mainlib/Foo.kt`, it checks `path.startsWith("/src/main/")` (false) and `path == "/src/main"` (false), correctly rejecting it. But the intent appears inconsistent—should exact match of the folder itself be allowed? If the goal is to match files *within* the folder, then `path == folderPrefix` should not pass. Consider whether the exact equality check is intentional or if only paths with the trailing separator should match.
2026-05-04T14:40:08.7433651Z     - In index-store/src/main/kotlin/io/github/amichne/kast/indexstore/FileFilterSpec.kt, lines 6-8: The example glob pattern `**Test.kt` is missing a forward slash separator. The correct pattern should be `**/*Test.kt` to match any file ending in `Test.kt` at any depth, as shown in the test cases.

2026-05-04T14:40:24.3747036Z 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3748242Z copilot: Two issues to fix: (1) remove the suspicious `path == folderPrefix` exact-match branch, (2) fix the docstring example glob pattern.
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3748821Z 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3748920Z copilot: The code review found two issues:
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3749822Z 1. The folder prefix exact match (`path == folderPrefix`) may be intended for directories but shouldn't apply to file paths. Files within a folder should match, but the folder itself as a "path" shouldn't match (unless it's somehow a file with the same name as the folder, which is unusual). I should remove the exact equality check.
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3750807Z 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3751079Z 2. The docstring example shows `**Test.kt` but it should be `**/*Test.kt`. This is just a doc issue.
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3751825Z 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3751926Z Let me fix both issues:
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3752545Z 1. Remove the `path == folderPrefix` check - if the intent is to filter files in a folder, only paths *starting with* `folderPrefix/` should match
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3753062Z 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3753067Z 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3753179Z 2. Fix the doc comment example
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3753351Z 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.3754832Z For the first issue, I'm reconsidering the logic. The use case is filtering metrics for files within a specific folder like `/src/main`, so a file path like `/src/main/Foo.kt` should match the `/src/main/` prefix. The folder path itself would never be a valid file path, so the exact equality check is unnecessary and represents a subtle correctness issue. I'll remove it and verify the existing tests still pass with the prefix-only approach.
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4284299Z 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4284811Z function:
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4285087Z   name: view
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4285332Z   args:
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4285952Z     path: /home/runner/work/kast/kast/index-store/src/main/kotlin/io/github/amichne/kast/indexstore/FileFilterSpec.kt
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4286639Z   result: |
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4286929Z     1. package io.github.amichne.kast.indexstore
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4287289Z     2. 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4287548Z     3. import java.nio.file.FileSystems
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4287863Z     4. 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4288052Z     5. /**
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4288318Z     6.  * Generic file-path filter for metrics queries.
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4288629Z     7.  *
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4288993Z     8.  * [fileGlob] is a glob pattern applied to the full file path (e.g. `**Test.kt`).
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4289586Z     9.  * [folderPrefix] is a path prefix filter - only files whose path starts with this
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4290077Z     10.  * prefix are included (e.g. `/src/main/kotlin`).
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4290408Z     11.  *
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4290735Z     12.  * Both filters are combined with AND semantics when both are non-null.
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4291120Z     13.  */
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4291507Z     14. data class FileFilterSpec(
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4291823Z     15.     val fileGlob: String? = null,
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4292180Z     16.     val folderPrefix: String? = null,
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4292503Z     17. ) {
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4292761Z     18.     private val globMatcher by lazy {
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4293059Z     19.         fileGlob?.let {
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4293489Z     20.             val pattern = if (it.startsWith("glob:") || it.startsWith("regex:")) it else "glob:$it"
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4294066Z     21.             FileSystems.getDefault().getPathMatcher(pattern)
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4294460Z     22.         }
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4294688Z     23.     }
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4294905Z     24. 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4295228Z     25.     /** Returns true if [path] passes all configured filters. */
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4295674Z     26.     fun matches(path: String?): Boolean {
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4296037Z     27.         if (path == null) return false
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4296386Z     28.         if (folderPrefix != null) {
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4296913Z     29.             val normalized = if (folderPrefix.endsWith("/")) folderPrefix else "$folderPrefix/"
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4297930Z     30.             if (!path.startsWith(normalized) && path != folderPrefix) return false
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4298394Z     31.         }
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4298653Z     32.         if (globMatcher != null) {
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4299043Z     33.             val filePath = java.nio.file.Path.of(path)
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4299496Z     34.             if (!globMatcher!!.matches(filePath)) return false
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4299874Z     35.         }
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4300114Z     36.         return true
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4300380Z     37.     }
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4300599Z     38. 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4300968Z     39.     val isEmpty: Boolean get() = fileGlob == null && folderPrefix == null
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4301748Z     40. }
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4301956Z     41. 
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4302441Z     42. /** Filters a list retaining only items where [pathExtractor] returns a path that passes [filter]. */
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4303263Z     43. fun <T> List<T>.filterByPath(filter: FileFilterSpec, pathExtractor: (T) -> String?): List<T> =
2026-05-04T14:40:24.4304019Z     44.     if (filter.isEmpty) this else filter { item -> filter.matches(pathExtractor(item)) }

2026-05-04T14:41:49.4507168Z        * [folderPrefix] is a path prefix filter - only files whose path starts with this

2026-05-04T14:41:49.4513209Z           fun matches(path: String?): Boolean {
2026-05-04T14:41:49.4513755Z               if (path == null) return false
2026-05-04T14:41:49.4514061Z               if (folderPrefix != null) {

2026-05-04T14:41:49.4516138Z                   val filePath = java.nio.file.Path.of(path)
2026-05-04T14:41:49.4516578Z                   if (!globMatcher!!.matches(filePath)) return false
2026-05-04T14:41:49.4516936Z               }
2026-05-04T14:41:49.4517193Z               return true
2026-05-04T14:41:49.4517451Z           }

2026-05-04T14:41:49.4520593Z           if (filter.isEmpty) this else filter { item -> filter.matches(pathExtractor(item)) }

2026-05-04T14:44:58.6381499Z copilot: All changes are implemented and tested. Here's a summary of what was done:
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6381943Z 
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6382035Z ---
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6382161Z 
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6382254Z ## Summary
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6382380Z 
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6382531Z ### 1. Removed git hash from index database path
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6384104Z `WorkspaceDirectoryResolver.workspaceDataDirectory()` — the git-remote branch no longer appends `workspaceHash(normalizedRoot)`. Database paths are now stable and predictable: `configHome/workspaces/{host}/{owner}/{repo}`.
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6384836Z 
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6384959Z ### 2. Workspace root defaults to current working directory
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6385711Z `CliCommandParser.requireWorkspaceRootPath()` now falls back to `System.getProperty("user.dir")` instead of throwing when `--workspace-root` is omitted. All metrics subcommands benefit automatically.
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6386440Z 
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6386568Z ### 3. Generic file filtering for metrics
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6386948Z New `FileFilterSpec` data class in `index-store` with:
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6387586Z - `fileGlob: String?` — glob pattern applied to the full file path (e.g. `**/*Test.kt`)
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6388253Z - `folderPrefix: String?` — prefix filter (e.g. `/src/main/kotlin`)
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6388770Z - `filterByPath<T>` extension function for list post-filtering
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6389091Z 
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6389207Z Filtering is threaded end-to-end:
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6390075Z - `MetricsEngine` methods `fanInRanking`, `fanOutRanking`, `lowUsageSymbols`, `deadCodeCandidates`, `changeImpactRadius` all accept `filter: FileFilterSpec = FileFilterSpec()`
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6390943Z - `KastMetricsRequest` / `KastMetricsQuery` gain `fileGlob` and `folderFilter` fields
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6391599Z - `CliCommand.Metrics` gains `fileGlob` and `folderFilter` fields  
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6392014Z - CLI: `--file-glob` and `--folder` options on all metrics subcommands
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6392457Z - `SkillWrapperExecutor` and `CliExecution` both build and pass `FileFilterSpec` to the engine
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6392777Z 
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6392878Z ### Tests added
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6393173Z - `FileFilterSpecTest` — 8 tests covering all combinations
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6393972Z - `MetricsEngineTest` — 4 new filtering tests (fanIn folder, fanIn glob, fanOut folder, deadCode folder)
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6394598Z - `CliCommandParserTest` — CWD default test for metrics without `--workspace-root`
2026-05-04T14:44:58.6395002Z - `KastConfigTest` — updated hash-removal assertion

2026-05-04T14:45:08.4370802Z   +package io.github.amichne.kast.indexstore
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4371013Z   +
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4371244Z   +import java.nio.file.FileSystems
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4371572Z   +
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4371702Z   +/**
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4371909Z   + * Generic file-path filter for metrics queries.
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4372187Z   + *
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4372580Z   + * [fileGlob] is a glob pattern applied to the full file path (e.g. `**&#47;*Test.kt`).
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4373244Z   + * [folderPrefix] is a path prefix filter - only files whose path starts with this
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4373776Z   + * prefix are included (e.g. `/src/main/kotlin`).
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4374107Z   + *
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4374437Z   + * Both filters are combined with AND semantics when both are non-null.
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4374857Z   + */
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4375082Z   +data class FileFilterSpec(
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4375387Z   +    val fileGlob: String? = null,
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4375708Z   +    val folderPrefix: String? = null,
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4376169Z   +) {
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4376402Z   +    private val globMatcher by lazy {
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4376720Z   +        fileGlob?.let {
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4377157Z   +            val pattern = if (it.startsWith("glob:") || it.startsWith("regex:")) it else "glob:$it"
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4377576Z   +            FileSystems.getDefault().getPathMatcher(pattern)
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4377940Z   +        }
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4378097Z   +    }
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4378234Z   +
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4378439Z   +    /** Returns true if [path] passes all configured filters. */
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4378798Z   +    fun matches(path: String?): Boolean {
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4379018Z   +        if (path == null) return false
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4379231Z   +        if (folderPrefix != null) {
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4379560Z   +            val normalized = if (folderPrefix.endsWith("/")) folderPrefix else "$folderPrefix/"
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4379917Z   +            if (!path.startsWith(normalized)) return false
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4380146Z   +        }
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4380300Z   +        if (globMatcher != null) {
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4380533Z   +            val filePath = java.nio.file.Path.of(path)
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4380802Z   +            if (!globMatcher!!.matches(filePath)) return false
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4381029Z   +        }
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4381173Z   +        return true
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4381460Z   +    }
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4381604Z   +
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4381823Z   +    val isEmpty: Boolean get() = fileGlob == null && folderPrefix == null
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4382086Z   +}
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4382218Z   +
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4382505Z   +/** Filters a list retaining only items where [pathExtractor] returns a path that passes [filter]. */
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4382987Z   +fun <T> List<T>.filterByPath(filter: FileFilterSpec, pathExtractor: (T) -> String?): List<T> =
2026-05-04T14:45:08.4383425Z   +    if (filter.isEmpty) this else filter { item -> filter.matches(pathExtractor(item)) }

2026-05-04T14:45:26.4494913Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4495436Z copilot: <pr_title>
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4495946Z feat(metrics): file filtering, CWD workspace default, simplified index path
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4496380Z </pr_title>
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4496476Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4496549Z <pr_description>
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4497730Z Metrics lacked any way to scope results to a subset of files; workspace root required explicit `--workspace-root` even when the intent is obvious from context; and the index DB path embedded an opaque workspace hash that made the storage layout unpredictable across invocations.
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4498556Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4498620Z ## Changes
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4498751Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4499112Z ### Index DB path — remove git hash segment
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4507475Z - `WorkspaceDirectoryResolver.workspaceDataDirectory()` for git-remote workspaces now resolves to `configHome/workspaces/{host}/{owner}/{repo}` instead of appending `workspaceHash(root)`. Path is now stable and human-readable.
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4508871Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4509005Z ### Workspace root defaults to CWD
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4509892Z - `CliCommandParser.requireWorkspaceRootPath()` falls back to `System.getProperty("user.dir")` instead of throwing. All metrics subcommands pick this up automatically.
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4511001Z - `SkillWrapperInput.resolveWorkspaceRoot()` already had this behaviour; CLI now aligns with it.
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4511599Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4511789Z ### Generic file filtering for metrics (`FileFilterSpec`)
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4512299Z New `FileFilterSpec` in `index-store` with AND-combined `fileGlob` + `folderPrefix` filtering:
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4512582Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4512644Z ```kotlin
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4512794Z // CLI
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4513141Z kast metrics fan-in --folder=/src/main/kotlin --file-glob="**/*Service.kt"
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4513360Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4513430Z // Skill wrapper
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4513696Z { "metric": "fanIn", "folderFilter": "/src/main/kotlin", "fileGlob": "**/*Service.kt" }
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4514108Z ```
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4514188Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4514797Z - `MetricsEngine.fanInRanking`, `fanOutRanking`, `lowUsageSymbols`, `deadCodeCandidates`, `changeImpactRadius` all accept `filter: FileFilterSpec = FileFilterSpec()` (opt-in, zero behaviour change when omitted).
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4515713Z - `filterByPath<T>` extension short-circuits on empty filter (no allocation overhead for the common case).
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4516439Z - `KastMetricsRequest`/`KastMetricsQuery` gain `fileGlob`/`folderFilter` fields; threaded through `SkillWrapperExecutor` and `CliExecution`.
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4517303Z - Module-level metrics (`coupling`, `cycles`, `moduleDepth`) are unaffected — they have no per-file paths.
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4517697Z 
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4517771Z ### Tests
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4518168Z - `FileFilterSpecTest` (8 cases): glob, folder prefix, AND semantics, null path, exact-prefix boundary.
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4518779Z - `MetricsEngineTest`: 4 new filtering cases (fanIn folder, fanIn glob, fanOut folder, deadCode folder).
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4519277Z - `CliCommandParserTest`: CWD default assertion for metrics without `--workspace-root`.
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4519695Z - `KastConfigTest`: updated path assertion to reflect hash removal.
2026-05-04T14:45:26.4519982Z </pr_description>

2026-05-04T14:50:05.3126936Z ##[warning]Node.js 20 actions are deprecated. The following actions are running on Node.js 20 and may not work as expected: actions/cache@v4, actions/setup-java@v4. Actions will be forced to run with Node.js 24 by default starting June 2nd, 2026. Node.js 20 will be removed from the runner on September 16th, 2026. Please check if updated versions of these actions are available that support Node.js 24. To opt into Node.js 24 now, set the FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true environment variable on the runner or in your workflow file. Once Node.js 24 becomes the default, you can temporarily opt out by setting ACTIONS_ALLOW_USE_UNSECURE_NODE_VERSION=true. For more information see: https://github.blog/changelog/2025-09-19-deprecation-of-node-20-on-github-actions-runners/
